query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "rynnworld-4d: 4d embodied world models for robotic manipulation" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) as it investigates the same core mechanism (world-action models for robotic manipulation) and domain (sim-to-real transfer in robotics) as the user's question, even though it focuses on the specific challenge of transfer rather than the 4D extension itself. It provides the necessary empirical baseline and canonical context for "world models" in robotic manipulation that a literature review on extending this technology would require.
